body.body:
Surfaced by T-audit-add-checks (#446) on first run of the new primary-classes-must-inherit-igorbase registered check. **105 classes** in wild_igor/igor/{cognition,memory,tools,network,brainstem}/ don't inherit from IgorBase.

## Why this matters

D125 (closed earlier today as resolved:implemented) said: 'Global base class for all Igor objects; moves diagnostics, monitoring, and common concerns onto one substrate.' But while IgorBase exists and 22 subclasses use it, 105 OTHER classes don't. The diagnostics/timer/logging substrate isn't actually uniform — it's spotty.

## Pattern of drift

Largely subclass chains where the PARENT class doesn't inherit IgorBase, so all its children also don't:
- **EmitChannel + 7 children** (BasketChannel, EmotionalMilieuChannel, CognitiveMilieuChannel, ConsoleChannel, WebChannel, DiscordChannel, MemoryChannel) — fix EmitChannel and all 7 cascade
- **InhibitionNode + 4 children** (TWMCheckNode, InferenceCheckNode, EstimateCheckNode, ActionGateNode) + InhibitionChain
- **BaseInterruptor + N children** (BudgetInterruptor, ContextInterruptor, MilieuInterruptor, ...)
- **EmitChannelRegistry, _RouterShim, DaemonSupervisor** — standalone classes

Fixing the parent of each chain probably cuts the count from 105 to ~20.

## Approach

1. **Inventory pass (Igor task per delegate-research-to-igor rule):** Igor walks the 105 violations and groups them by inheritance chain. Reports each chain root + child count. Identifies any classes that genuinely shouldn't be IgorBase (data containers, registries, exception subclasses).
2. **Fix parents (CC sprint):** For each chain root, add IgorBase as a base. Run tests after each fix. Each chain root fix should drop multiple violations.
3. **Fix standalones:** The non-chain classes either get IgorBase or get explicitly acked (via audit_add.py ack) with a documented reason.
4. **Re-run check:** verify count drops to zero or to acked-only count.

## Existing work to check

- wild_igor/igor/igor_base.py — the IgorBase class
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/emit_channels.py — biggest single chain (8 classes)
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/inhibition_chain.py — second biggest (6 classes)
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/interruptors.py — BaseInterruptor chain
- lab/claudecode/audit_check_igorbase.py — the check that surfaced this
- lab/claudecode/audit_add.py — for ack-ing legitimate exemptions

## Out of scope

- Restructuring class hierarchies beyond adding IgorBase
- Removing IgorBase from anywhere (the rule is ADD, not migrate)
- Refactoring the chain root parents (just add IgorBase to existing class signature)

## Provenance

T-audit-add-checks first run 2026-04-14. The audit-add infrastructure works — this is the kind of finding it exists to surface. The check is a forever check so reintroduction of D125 drift will be caught in future sweeps.
body.title: D125 drift — 105 primary classes don't inherit from IgorBase
body.result: 41 classes now inherit IgorBase. Bootstrap PathManager split resolves circular import. Dynamic __class__ mixin on promotion. Skipped ebook_drm + brainstem. All classes get: instance naming, lazy logger, perf timing, dump().
